theme: Batch tasks by hidden chain structure
title: Foundry Line Batching: Track Assignment Under a Hidden Chain Ceiling
strategies: trivial: reproduce the checker's own baseline -- the k individually most profitable tasks, each alone on its own line

greedy: sort all tasks by profit descending, first-fit-append each onto the first production line it is precedence-consistent with (the trap -- cannot see that mutually-incomparable high-profit tasks each burn a whole line)

strong: compute a minimum chain (path) cover of the whole precedence poset via maximum bipartite matching on the reachability graph (Dilworth/Fulkerson duality), trim each recovered chain to its top-H-profit order-preserving subsequence, then keep the min(k, #chains) most valuable trimmed chains
invalid: dump every task onto one unordered line, violating both the horizon cap and precedence
